description: Validate that agent or controller actions cause rigid-body motion through an explicit action trace, contact/impulse evidence, and post-action trajectory response. Pushing a box or throwing a ball are smoke families, not separate core capabilities.
physical_assumptions: Agent actions are structured action trace events, not hidden keyframes.
Action-coupled rigid bodies start with zero unexplained motion.
Rigid-body response must happen after an agent action and matching contact or impulse evidence.
Push actions require contact evidence; throw/release actions require an impulse or release label.
The agent may be kinematic or skeletal, but the target rigid body must remain physics-critical.
verifier_rules: Action trace contains actor_id, target_id, action_type, frame, and time_s.
Action-coupled target starts below velocity epsilon.
Target displacement and velocity before first action stay below epsilon.
Push actions include agent-target contact evidence near the action frame.
Throw/release actions include impulse or release metadata.
Target moves after the action above the expected threshold.
repair_suggestions: Write structured action_trace events instead of relying on prompt prose or animation.
Clear target initial velocity before the action frame.
Bind the target as a physics-critical rigid body with collider and collision profile.
Export agent-target contact for push actions or impulse/release labels for throw actions.
Move post-action target motion into runtime physics instead of keyframing.
